events.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
events.description: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

events.title: Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries
events.description: A growing share of global hunger is becoming entrenched in a small group of conflict-hit countries, with two-thirds of people facing acute food insecurity concentrated in just 10 nations, a major international report backed by UN agencies warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries

events.title: Extreme heat pushing global food systems to the brink, UN agencies warn
events.description: Extreme heat is pushing global food and farming systems to the brink, threatening the livelihoods of over a billion people as rising temperatures and more frequent heatwaves redefine how food is produced worldwide, a new UN report warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Extreme heat pushing global food systems to the brink, UN agencies warn

events.title: Syrian authorities arrest ex-intelligence officer tied to a leaked video of a massacre
events.description: Syrian authorities have arrested a former intelligence officer allegedly linked to a video showing the fatal shooting of dozens during the country's conflict
events.ticker_label: 💥 Syrian authorities arrest ex-intelligence officer tied to a leaked video of a massacre

events.title: French foreign minister Barrot voices concern about 'terrorist threat' in the Sahel
events.description: France's foreign minister Jean-Noël Barrot on Friday voiced concern about the spread of Islamist extremism in west Africa, after talks with Togo's leader, Faure Gnassingbe. Violence by jihadist groups has blighted the Sahel region for the last decade, and is now spreading into northern regions of coastal countries such as Togo.
events.ticker_label: 💥 French foreign minister Barrot voices concern about 'terrorist threat' in the Sahel

events.title: A mass trial in El Salvador and other top photos from Latin America and the Caribbean
events.description: April 17–23, 2026 Alleged gang members were put on mass trial at the Terrorist Confinement Center, or CECOT, in Tecoluca, El Salvador. A gunman fired at tourists from atop one of Mexico's Teotihuacan pyramids, killing a Canadian person and wounded others. ___ This gallery was curated by photographer Dolores Ochoa, based in Quito, Ecuador. ___ AP photography: https://apnews.com/photography Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/apnews
events.ticker_label: 💥 A mass trial in El Salvador and other top photos from Latin America and the Caribbean

events.title: EU leaders discuss Iran crisis in Cyprus
events.description: European leaders meeting in Cyprus have pledged to step up efforts to help resolve conflicts in the Middle East, as the United States continues to criticize Europe for what it sees as insufficient support in countering Iran, with the EU largely remaining on the sidelines, while French President Emmanuel Macron stressed that restoring stability quickly is in everyone’s interest and key to reassuring global economies, ahead of talks with regional partners from Egypt, Lebanon and Syria and remarks from EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas. France24 journalist Shirli Sitbon tells us more.

events.title: Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch
events.description: The shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz caused by war in the Middle East has exposed a new threat: a looming shortage of strategic minerals that drive economies all over the world – and a race by countries to obtain them.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch

events.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
events.description: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands The former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte , will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”. Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes against humanity of murder and attempted murder in relation to anti-drugs crackdowns that led to the killing of thousands of people. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity

events.title: World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update
events.description: Continued strikes, shelling and gunfire in residential areas of Gaza are raising alarm over civilian safety, the UN relief coordination office OCHA has warned.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition
events.description: The Security Council is meeting now at a critical juncture for Syria’s fragile transition. While the Iran-US ceasefire has temporarily eased regional spillover from Lebanon and elsewhere, conditions remain volatile in Syria. The reported US military withdrawal now completed, marks a milestone, yet the chamber faces a country grappling with deep internal fissures and external pressures. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users should click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition

events.title: How frustration at Cop stalemates inspires first global talks on phasing out fossil fuels
events.description: ‘Coalition of the willing’ gathers in Colombia to try to bypass petrostate blockages of Cop summits and chart fresh path The world’s first Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels conference, co-hosted by Colombia and the Netherlands, takes place in Santa Marta, Colombia, from 24 to 29 April. A “coalition of the willing” – including 54 countries and various subnational governments, civil society groups and academics – will try to chart a new path to powering the world with low-carbon energy. Continue reading...

events.title: 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster
events.description: This Sunday marks 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster, which the UN says affected more than 3.5 million people and contaminated nearly 50,000 square kilometres of land, and which remains the worst nuclear accident in history, as France Télévisions gains rare access to the site for a closer look.

events.title: India-Pakistan ties 'not good', a year after Kashmir attack
events.description: A year after the deadly terror attack in Kashmir, relations between India and Pakistan remain at an all-time low. Since then, Islamabad has emerged as a key mediator seeking to end the war in Iran. On Access Asia, we're joined by Indian MP Dr Shashi Tharoor to find out what India makes of Pakistan's push for peace.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India-Pakistan ties 'not good', a year after Kashmir attack

events.title: 'The world bailed out the banks, no one is bailing out the children,' UNICEF says
events.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, UNICEF global spokesperson James Elder discussed the situation in Lebanon, where "critical infrastructure that girls and boys rely on" have been targeted, giving Lebanese people "a sense that there really is nowhere safe". He also declared that humanitarian groups like UNICEF are facing an "existential funding crisis", regretting that "the world bailed out the banks" but "no one is bailing out the children".

events.title: Despite ceasefire, Hormuz tensions continue to throttle supply chains worldwide
events.description: A temporary ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been extended, offering an opening for diplomacy – but persistent tensions in the Strait of Hormuz are compounding trade disruptions and intensifying pressure on humanitarian operations and vulnerable communities far beyond the Gulf.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Despite ceasefire, Hormuz tensions continue to throttle supply chains worldwide

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence
events.description: The Security Council is addressing the deepening crisis in Haiti on Thursday, centered on the Secretary-General’s latest report which highlights a security landscape of both intensified enforcement and rising civilian risk. While operations including by the UN-backed Gang Suppression Force (GSF) between December and February resulted in the deaths of 1,343 suspected gang members, the humanitarian cost remains staggering. The UN Special Representative told ambassadors elections are the “only legitimate path” back to political stability, while his counterpart with the GSF said its success depends on “effective coordination”. Follow full meetings coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 💥 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence

events.title: Iran-Iraq Tanker War redux? Why the Strait of Hormuz crisis is different
events.description: The Tanker War between Iran and Iraq in the 1980s also disrupted global shipping. But the situation today is different.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran-Iraq Tanker War redux? Why the Strait of Hormuz crisis is different

events.title: Russia kills two prisoners in their home
events.description: In Ukraine, a Russian drone attack in the coastal city of Odesa killed a married couple in their seventies when their home was struck early in the morning, with several apartment buildings damaged—one nearly split in two—while separate drone strikes in the frontline region of Kherson also left two dead, a 68-year-old man and a woman whose identity was still being confirmed, according to local officials, in a report by Catherine Viette.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia kills two prisoners in their home

events.title: Long hours, stress and harassment are causing hundreds of thousands of early deaths, says UN labour agency
events.description: More than 840,000 people die each year from health conditions linked to risks such as long working hours, job insecurity, workplace harassment and bullying, according to a new report by the International Labour Organization (ILO).

events.title: At least 1 dead, 5 wounded after shooting inside state's largest shopping mall
events.description: A shooting was reported at Louisiana’s largest shopping center, the Mall of Louisiana, and officials said at least one person is dead and five others are wounded.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 At least 1 dead, 5 wounded after shooting inside state's largest shopping mall

events.title: PM speaks to Vietnamese president on supply chains – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Allegra Spender says 25% gas export tax would help fix ‘faulty’ system Independent MP Allegra Spender said a 25% tax on gas exports would help rectify what she sees as “faulty” taxation arrangements that have seen an Australian resource sent overseas with minimal benefit to the country. The gas industry is a very profitable industry and pays income tax. And every company in Australia, frankly, should pay income tax on its profits and should pay the proper rate. But the gas companies are different because they also sell an Australian resource which they extract, which we can’t get back once it is sold. I think Australians rightly believe they should share more of that revenue. We’re back here again and they should fix it. We are lucky to be an energy exporter at a time where the world needs energy. We are a great partner in this. But it is a reasonable thing for Australians to get a fair return on that. And, at the moment, we just aren’t. We can’t and I think we need to be really honest about that. If there’s going to be changes to the NDIS – and I’m not a state leader who’s knocking the federal government’s right and probably responsibility to reform the NDIS, it’s cost too much money – but we have to be really frank with people. We can’t offer at the state level the kinds of services that are being rolled out at the NDIS. Continue reading...

events.title: Nuclear experts warn Iran’s uranium ‘right’ is a myth, say Trump is right to hold firm
events.description: Experts say the U.S. must permanently ban Iran's uranium enrichment program, warning Tehran could resume its nuclear weapons path if infrastructure stays.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Nuclear experts warn Iran’s uranium ‘right’ is a myth, say Trump is right to hold firm

events.description: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

events.description: Italy's government has ruled out any suggestion the country could replace Iran at this summer's World Cup due to the war in the Middle East.

events.description: Ukraine has secured a crucial European Union loan to support the country's wartime efforts

events.title: Jean Lafaurie, 102 years old, still resisting
events.description: Deported to Dachau during WWII, Resistance fighter Jean Lafaurie survived the hell of the concentration camp thanks to the solidarity among prisoners. At 102 years old, he continues to share his story. On France's National Day of Remembrance for the Victims of Deportation, he issues a warning in defence of democracy and freedom.Video by StéphanieTrouillard and Claire Paccalin.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jean Lafaurie, 102 years old, still resisting

events.title: US Defense Secretary Hegseth: 'US blockade has gone global'
events.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Operation Epic Fury has achieved a decisive result in just weeks, focused on preventing Iran from developing nuclear weapons. He noted the mission is entering a new phase, giving Iran the chance to pursue a “good and wise” peace deal, while emphasising the strength of the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, soon to be reinforced by a second aircraft carrier. France24 journalist Shirli Sitbon shares further details on the press conference.

events.title: Fleeing war in Sudan, refugees flock to Libya's Kufra province
events.description: For the past three years, war has been raging in Sudan betwe en the regular army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces, leading to more than 11 million Sudanese being displaced. After Chad, Libya is the largest host country of Sudanese refugees. In the southeastern Kufra province, 250 kilometres from the Sudanese border, between 40 000 and 60 000 Sudanese refugees live either in encampments or towns. FRANCE 24's Lilia Blaise and Hamdi Tlili report.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fleeing war in Sudan, refugees flock to Libya's Kufra province

events.title: Ceasefire extension offers diplomatic opening, but tensions persist in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The United States’ decision to extend a fragile ceasefire with Iran has kept a narrow window open for diplomacy, but fresh security incidents in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday underscore the volatility of the situation and the risks to global shipping and regional stability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ceasefire extension offers diplomatic opening, but tensions persist in Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Florida officials investigate planned ‘Sloth World’ attraction after 31 sloths die in warehouse
events.description: Languorous tree dwellers from Guyana and Peru died from ‘cold stun’ in warehouse with no power or running water Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Wildlife officials in Florida said in a newly released report that dozens of sloths taken from South American rainforests for display at a controversial new tourist attraction in Orlando died in the care of their new owners. An incident report from the Florida fish and wildlife conservation commission (FWC) said that 31 of the mammals procured from Peru and Guyana by the owners of a forthcoming attraction called Sloth World perished in a storage warehouse more than a year ago, between December 2024 and February 2025. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Florida officials investigate planned ‘Sloth World’ attraction after 31 sloths die in warehouse

events.title: Embassy encourages Australia-based Iranians to volunteer to help war effort
events.description: Message on Telegram account has sparked alarm among diaspora groups, with one calling for the embassy to be closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tehran’s embassy in Canberra is promoting a recruitment drive for a paramilitary campaign to defend Iran from US forces, with the Australian federal police now looking into the matter. As the war approaches the two-month mark, the embassy’s website and Telegram channel this week posted a message that instructed people on how to register for a voluntary drive to fight for their country , known as the “Janfada” – or “sacrificing life” – program. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Embassy encourages Australia-based Iranians to volunteer to help war effort

events.title: Libya: UN mission chief laments lack of progress towards national renewal

events.title: Cuba's ambassador to Mexico says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis

events.title: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
events.description: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking points Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry. It’s understood the government has elected not to pursue a new tax on gas exports in the budget , prompted in part by the global oil crisis and Anthony Albanese’s diplomatic efforts in shoring up fuel supply from Asian allies by pledging reliable access to liquefied natural gas. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax

events.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
events.description: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said. Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Kiat told Reuters in an interview on Tuesday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex

events.title: Amid fuel crisis, a bold move to leave oil and gas behind
events.description: At an unprecedented meeting in Colombia, delegates from more than 50 countries want to chart a practical path to wean the world off fossil fuels. Can they succeed where the UN climate talks have failed?

events.title: WHO says billions saw health gains in 2025 despite funding cuts
events.description: Despite significant funding cuts, the World Health Organization (WHO) was able to support significant national health gains for hundreds of millions of people in 2025, according to its annual Results Report released on Thursday.

events.title: Ceasefire extended: What's next in the Iran war?

events.description: In the 1990s, just after the fall of the Berlin Wall, the former East Germany experienced a wave of far-right racist and political violence. In Germany, this period is known as the "baseball bat years" – a dark period that still haunts the country today.

events.title: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo
events.description: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the US The Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday. events.title: Why are so many Colombians fighting for Ukraine?
events.description: On the most dangerous parts of Ukraine's front line, it's almost as common these days to hear Spanish as Ukrainian. Many Colombian soldiers are now fighting in Ukraine, lured by the comparatively high salaries, but also by a sense of adventure and of helping a good cause. Our reporters Gulliver Cragg and Catalina Gomez travelled around Colombia and Ukraine to understand what drives these men to go to war and what their families think.

events.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments
events.description: The former ballet dancer said Chalamet’s comments were ‘reckless’ in an interview with the New York Times in which she also discussed her violent childhood Actor and former ballet dancer Charlize Theron has joined the chorus of disapproval aimed at Timothée Chalamet over his remarks that appeared to disrespect performers of ballet and opera . In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms that we need to lift up constantly because, yes, they do have a hard time. But in 10 years, AI is going to be able to do Timothée’s job, but it will not be able to replace a person on a stage dancing live.” Continue reading...

events.title: Soundtrack of the sea: divers use underwater speakers to help dying coral reefs
events.description: Divers are installing waterproof speakers in the ocean to help pull a coral reef near Jamaica back from the brink The northern coast of Jamaica once served as the backdrop for scenes in the James Bond thriller No Time to Die. But today, beneath those same turquoise waves, a real-life mission is unfolding: the race to pull a dying coral reef back from the brink. However, the tools a team of divers are carrying to the seafloor are not what you would expect to find in a marine biologist’s kit. They are installing waterproof speakers at the bottom of the ocean, and the man leading the team is not a scientist. Continue reading...

events.title: EU approves a $106 billion loan package to help Ukraine after Hungary lifts its veto
events.description: The European Union on Thursday approved a $106-billion loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for two years, ending months of political deadlock.

events.title: French police probe suspected weather device tampering after odd Polymarket bet
events.description: The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.

events.title: Germany news: Lawmakers vote to lower fuel tax amid high gas prices
events.description: People in Germany could see gas prices fall next week after lawmakers approved a bill to lower the fuel tax by €0.17 per liter. Follow DW for these stories and more.

events.title: Natural molecule could provide Ozempic, Wegovy alternative
events.description: Discovered using AI, a naturally occurring molecule appears to act directly in the brain's appetite center. Scientists hope it could refine treatments for obesity, offering fewer side effects for some patients.

events.title: Lebanon should be part of an Iran peace deal, EU's Ursula von der Leyen says
events.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said the past weeks had shown how "security is indivisible." She said a deal with Iran should include Lebanon and respect its "soverignty and territorial integrity."

events.title: Nepal: Mount Everest ice block obstructs large group of spring climbers
events.description: A chunk of ice suspended above the Khumbu Icefall near Base Camp on Mount Everest means it's not safe either for climbers or the specialist "icefall doctors" who typically clear away smaller obstructions.

events.title: Thai opposition figures face trial over attempt to change royal defamation law
events.description: Forty-four lawmakers could be banned from office for seeking to change Thailand's strict lese-majeste law, which criminalizes criticism of the monarchy. Critics say it has been used to silence opponents.

events.title: China's DeepSeek launches preview of new AI model
events.description: DeepSeek, the startup behind a low-cost chatbot that sent shockwaves through the AI community last year, has released its highly anticipated V4 model.

events.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up
events.description: Facebook's parent company said it would slash 10% of its workforce, while Microsoft is offering an early retirement scheme. The cuts come as both tech giants make massive investments in AI.

events.title: US soldier allegedly made $400k on Polymarket betting on Maduro raid
events.description: Prosecutors say the soldier used classified information of the Maduro operation to place winning bets on Polymarket. The case comes amid other incidents of prediction market bets tied to US government actions.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US soldier allegedly made $400k on Polymarket betting on Maduro raid

events.title: Streep, Hathaway and Blunt reunite for 'The Devil Wears Prada 2' premieres
events.description: In this edition of arts24, Hollywood nostalgia takes centre stage as Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway and Emily Blunt reunite for the long-awaited sequel to "The Devil Wears Prada", bringing high fashion to the red carpet.

events.title: 🔴Live: UN Indonesian peacekeeper dies of wounds suffered in Lebanon last month
events.description: No description available.

events.title: US soldier bet on Maduro capture on Polymarket
events.description: A US Special Forces soldier involved in the operation to capture Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro has been charged with multiple crimes, including unlawful use of classified government information, after allegedly using insider knowledge to place bets on Polymarket that earned him over $400,000. Prosecutors say the soldier, who had access to sensitive details about the mission, profited from wagers tied to the outcome of the operation and is now accused of wire fraud and other financial offences linked to the use of nonpublic information. Camille Knight has this story.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US soldier bet on Maduro capture on Polymarket

events.title: US soldier 'made hundreds of thousands of dollars betting on Maduro capture'
events.description: A US special forces soldier involved in the military operation that captured Nicolas Maduro has been charged with allegedly betting on the raid.

events.title: After last year's fighting with Thailand, Cambodia readies new conscription law for men aged 18-25
events.description: Cambodia's Cabinet has approved a law requiring males aged 18 to 25 to serve two years in the military

events.title: Kosovo court sentences 3 ethnic Serbs to life, 30 years in prison over 2023 clash
events.description: A court in Kosovo has sentenced two ethnic Serbs to life in prison and another to 30 years over a 2023 clash with police that left four people dead

events.title: Bill to allow assisted dying in England and Wales is set to fall as parliamentary time runs out
events.description: A proposed bill to allow terminally ill adults in England and Wales to choose to end their lives is set to fall Friday as parliamentary time runs out

events.title: Japan's Haruki Murakami to publish first new novel in 3 years with 'The Tale of KAHO' in July
events.description: A new book by Haruki Murakami set to be released in early July will mark the first time a full-length novel by the Japanese author features a female main character

events.title: Prosecutors seek 30-year prison term for South Korea's Yoon
events.description: South Korean prosecutors are requesting a 30-year prison term for ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol

events.title: China to send giant pandas to Atlanta again
events.description: China is sending two giant pandas to Zoo Atlanta, the China Wildlife Conservation Association says

events.title: South Africa's police chief suspended over corruption allegations
events.description: South Africa's top police official, Fannie Masemola, has been suspended by the president after being charged with breaking finance laws

events.title: New bridge helps cement Lesotho as water lifeline for South Africa’s economic hub
events.description: A newly built bridge in Lesotho is set to nearly double its water exports to South Africa

events.title: EU pulls $2.4 million from Venice Biennale over Russia's return
events.description: The Venice Biennale has lost 2-million-euro ($2.4 million) in funding due to its decision to allow Russia to participate in the 61st contemporary art show

events.title: Time to apply the brakes to runaway AI, says pioneer
events.description: If AI is “a very fast car with no steering wheel” then regulation must provide one, insists Nobel laureate and Artificial Intelligence pioneer Geoffrey Hinton, the visionary scientist widely known as the “godfather” of the self-learning tech.

events.title: Forensic genealogy unmasks cold case suspect as strangler, sexual predator decades later: officials
events.description: Authorities say advances in forensic genetic genealogy solved two cold cases in Brockton, Massachusetts, identifying a suspect decades after the crimes.

events.title: US economic chokehold on Iran reaches peak leverage and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Zodiac Killer may be tied to Black Dahlia case after ‘code cracked,’ new suspect emerges
events.description: An independent investigator claims he decoded the Zodiac Killer's cipher and found evidence allegedly connecting it to the 1947 Black Dahlia murder.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Zodiac Killer may be tied to Black Dahlia case after ‘code cracked,’ new suspect emerges

events.title: Elite school teacher known as 'Mr Wonderful' accused of heinous crimes against students
events.description: A former teacher who was nicknamed "Mr. Wonderful" has been charged with three counts of rape involving two former students who allege years of abuse and grooming.

events.title: Army soldier involved in Maduro capture arrested for allegedly using classified intel to win $410K in bets
events.description: A soldier involved in Nicolás Maduro's capture allegedly placed insider bets on Polymarket using classified information, winning nearly $410,000.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Army soldier involved in Maduro capture arrested for allegedly using classified intel to win $410K in bets

events.title: Decades-old cold case breakthrough leads to arrest in execution-style killings of married couple before child
events.description: A Florida man is accused of gunning down a couple execution-style in front of their 3-year-old in a 1993 Bronx cold case that haunted a family for decades.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Decades-old cold case breakthrough leads to arrest in execution-style killings of married couple before child

events.title: Sex offender accused of 6 sex assaults in one day as crime surge rocks affluent valley
events.description: Registered sex offender Damian Robinson accused of a string of daytime sexual assaults in Los Angeles has turned himself in, authorities said.

events.title: US economic chokehold on Iran reaches peak leverage as collapse risks emerge
events.description: U.S. economic pressure on Iran has reached historic levels, but inconsistent enforcement limits full impact of sanctions, a former Treasury expert says.

events.title: Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron
events.description: Analysts say the IRGC seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz in a tit-for-tat move after a U.S. naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian ship.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron

events.title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to face trial on crimes against humanity charges
events.description: The ICC confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for allegedly overseeing deadly drug crackdowns.

events.title: Trump envoy to Turkey doubles down after backlash, pushes ‘peace through strength’ policy
events.description: U.S. Ambassador to Turkey and Syria envoy Tom Barrack defends controversial remarks on Hezbollah, Turkey’s F-35 access and Middle East governance as pragmatic realism, not policy shifts.
